title: Sarcochilus zollingeri
taxonomy: Sarcochilus zollingeri is classified within the family Orchidaceae, specifically within the subfamily Cranichidoideae. This species belongs to the genus Sarcochilus, which is a group of orchids native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Sarcochilus zollingeri is further classified as a species within this genus, with the specific epithet 'zollingeri' referring to its discovery by the botanist Heinrich Zollinger. The species is native to Indonesia and Malaysia, where it grows in humid forests and mountainous regions. As a member of the orchid family, Sarcochilus zollingeri is a flowering plant characterized by its unique and often colorful blooms.

intro: Sarcochilus zollingeri, also known as the Zollinger's orchid, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sarcochilus zollingeri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sarcochilus zollingeri is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders, as well as to reduce fever and inflammation.
